verses.text: The LORD: depart, go up, thou and the people, to the land I swore to the fathers. I will send an angel; drive out the Canaanite — to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go in the midst of thee, thou art stiff-necked, lest I consume thee. Guidance: God offers them everything except Himself. The land, the angel, the victory — but His face withdrawn. Moses will not accept this. Israel has been offered an upgrade and a vacancy at the same time.

The LORD: depart, go up, thou and the people, to the land I swore to the fathers. I will send an angel; drive out the Canaanite — to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go in the midst of thee, thou art stiff-necked, lest I consume thee. Guidance: God offers them everything except Himself. The land, the angel, the victory — but His face withdrawn. Moses will not accept this. Israel has been offered an upgrade and a vacancy at the same time.

The LORD: depart, go up, thou and the people, to the land I swore to the fathers. I will send an angel; drive out the Canaanite — to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go in the midst of thee, thou art stiff-necked, lest I consume thee. Guidance: God offers them everything except Himself. The land, the angel, the victory — but His face withdrawn. Moses will not accept this. Israel has been offered an upgrade and a vacancy at the same time.

When the people heard, they mourned; no man put on his ornaments. The LORD: ye are stiff-necked; if I came up in your midst one moment I would consume you. Put off thy ornaments, that I may know what to do. Israel stripped themselves of ornaments by mount Horeb. Guidance: The same gold that became a calf is now stripped off in grief. Sometimes God removes our adornments to clarify identity. Bare and mourning, Israel is asking the right question for the first time.

Moses pitched the tabernacle outside the camp, called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. All who sought the LORD went out. When Moses went out, the people stood at their tent doors watching. The cloudy pillar descended; the LORD talked with him face to face, as a man to his friend. Joshua departed not from the tabernacle. Guidance: God speaks to Moses as a friend. While Moses is inside, Israel stands at every tent door. The camp is shaped by one man's nearness to God.

Moses: Thou sayest, bring up this people; but Thou hast not let me know whom Thou wilt send with me. Thou hast said, I know thee by name. If I have found grace, show me Thy way that I may know Thee. This nation is Thy people. Guidance: Moses asks not for marching orders but for God Himself. Show me Thy way — meaning, show me Thee. He gives God back the words: this nation is Thy people. Intercession is reminding God of His own promises.

Moses: Thou sayest, bring up this people; but Thou hast not let me know whom Thou wilt send with me. Thou hast said, I know thee by name. If I have found grace, show me Thy way that I may know Thee. This nation is Thy people. Guidance: Moses asks not for marching orders but for God Himself. Show me Thy way — meaning, show me Thee. He gives God back the words: this nation is Thy people. Intercession is reminding God of His own promises.

The LORD: My presence shall go with thee; I will give thee rest. Moses: if Thy presence go not, carry us not hence. How shall it be known I have found grace? Is it not in that Thou goest with us? So shall we be separated from all peoples. Guidance: Without His presence, the promised land is just real estate. Moses refuses to lead one step if God is not in the middle. The mark of God's people has never been their place — it has been His company.

The LORD: I will do this also, for thou hast found grace, and I know thee by name. Moses: show me Thy glory. The LORD: I will make all My goodness pass before thee; I will proclaim My name. I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious; show mercy to whom I will show mercy. Guidance: Moses' boldness keeps growing. He won God's presence; now he asks God's glory. God answers by promising goodness — and announcing sovereign mercy. Paul quotes this in Romans 9.

Thou canst not see My face — no man sees Me and lives. There is a place by Me; stand on a rock. As My glory passes, I put thee in a cleft, cover thee with My hand. I remove My hand; thou seest My back; My face shall not be seen. Guidance: Even Moses cannot see God's face directly. Hidden in a cleft, shown only His back. Paul says: in Christ's face we now see what Moses could not. Christ is the cleft and the glory at once.
